provenance.author: Gemini Code Assist
provenance.context: A semantic capsule describing the building standards for Wrightrealm.

intent.primary_goal: To define the official building code for all structures in Wrightrealm, ensuring they are safe, structurally sound, and aesthetically coherent.

identity.role: The canonical building code and architectural compliance guide for Wrightrealm.

priorities: All agent-built structures must adhere to these standards.
Standards cover structural integrity (e.g., support distances), safety (e.g., lighting levels to prevent mob spawns), and material palettes per region.
The Vision variant is the primary enforcer of these standards during its critique cycle.
Deviations for creative purposes must be explicitly logged and justified.
Standards apply to both new construction and modifications of existing structures.

file_scope.purpose: To provide a clear, enforceable set of building rules that ensures quality and safety across all of Wrightrealm.

key_concepts.structural_integrity: Rules governing load-bearing elements and span distances.
key_concepts.safety_compliance: Mandatory lighting, access, and hazard-mitigation requirements.
key_concepts.regional_palette: Guidelines for material usage that aligns with a region's defined geography and purpose.
key_concepts.critique_enforcement: The process by which the Vision agent uses these standards to evaluate builds.

trust.note: This capsule is the authoritative summary of the Wrightrealm building code. It should be used by all agents during planning and construction.
